questions.question:
A $4.10\text{ kg}$ bowling ball rolling at $6.30\text{ m/s}$ strikes a stationary $85.0\text{ kg}$ crate on a frictionless, icy surface and becomes securely embedded in it.

Calculate the final velocity of the combined system immediately following the collision.

A $0.45\text{ kg}$ puck sliding across an ice rink at $24.0\text{ m/s}$ is caught mid-air by an initially motionless $62.5\text{ kg}$ skater.

Determine the magnitude of the velocity of the skater and puck combination after the catch.

While standing still on a frictionless frozen pond, a $92.0\text{ kg}$ hockey player catches a $1.20\text{ kg}$ heavy training ball thrown directly toward him at a speed of $11.5\text{ m/s}$.

How fast do the player and ball move together after the interaction?

A $0.350\text{ kg}$ block is sliding on frictionless ice and strikes a stationary $4.20\text{ kg}$ heavy sled, sticking to it securely upon impact. The combined block-sled system moves off at a speed of $1.50\text{ m/s}$.

What was the initial speed of the sliding block?

While standing on a frictionless frozen lake, an initially motionless $82.0\text{ kg}$ park ranger catches a heavy emergency supply pack thrown directly at him. The ranger and the supply pack glide together at a speed of $0.850\text{ m/s}$ immediately after the catch, and the pack has a mass of $6.50\text{ kg}$.

Calculate the initial speed at which the pack was thrown.

A runaway $12.0\text{ kg}$ curling stone traveling across smooth ice hits a stationary $55.0\text{ kg}$ equipment crate and becomes wedged underneath it. The collision causes the combined stone and crate to slide forward at a velocity of $2.20\text{ m/s}$.

With what speed was the curling stone initially traveling?

A $2.20 \times 10^3\text{ kg}$ truck traveling East at $80.0\text{ km/h}$ collides head-on with an oncoming $1.10 \times 10^3\text{ kg}$ compact car traveling West at $90.0\text{ km/h}$. After the collision, the heavy truck continues moving East but slows down to $20.0\text{ km/h}$.

Determine the final velocity (speed and direction) of the compact car immediately after the impact.

A $1.35 \times 10^3\text{ kg}$ sedan heading North at $110\text{ km/h}$ has a head-on collision with a $1.80 \times 10^3\text{ kg}$ SUV traveling South at $75.0\text{ km/h}$. Following the crash, the sedan is thrown backward, traveling South at $15.0\text{ km/h}$.

What is the final velocity of the heavier SUV after the collision?

A $9.50 \times 10^2\text{ kg}$ electric sports car traveling West at $120\text{ km/h}$ collides head-on with a stationary $2.40 \times 10^3\text{ kg}$ delivery van idling at a crosswalk. Immediately after the crash, the sports car rebounds completely backward, moving East at $40.0\text{ km/h}$.

Calculate the resulting speed and direction of the delivery van after the impact.

A $58.0\text{ kg}$ astronaut is floating motionless in deep space during a spacewalk. To return toward her spacecraft, she throws a heavy $6.20\text{ kg}$ tool kit away from the ship at a velocity of $4.80\text{ m/s}$.

What is the resulting velocity of the astronaut?
questions.explanation: $0 = m_av_a + m_tv_t$. $0 = 58.0v_a + (6.20)(4.80)$. $0 = 58.0v_a + 29.76$. $v_a = -0.513\text{ m/s}$ — opposite direction to the throw, toward the ship.

questions.question:
A security guard carrying a $6.20\text{ kg}$ defense weapon loaded with a $0.120\text{ kg}$ tracking projectile is standing on a flatbed cargo truck moving West at a steady speed of $45.0\text{ km/h}$. The guard fires the weapon directly West, forcing the projectile out at a speed of $180\text{ m/s}$ relative to the truck.

Determine the resulting velocity of the weapon relative to the ground in kilometers per hour.

A physics student holding an adjusted $3.50\text{ kg}$ spring-loaded launcher containing a $0.220\text{ kg}$ steel ball sits on a flat luggage cart rolling North at $12.0\text{ km/h}$. The student fires the launcher, sending the ball North at a speed of $25.0\text{ m/s}$ relative to the moving cart.

Find the velocity of the launcher relative to the ground immediately after firing.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered complex conservation of momentum applications!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review problems involving direction changes and relative velocity conversions.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on sign conventions for direction and setting up $m_1v_{1i} + m_2v_{2i} = m_1v_{1f} + m_2v_{2f}$.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: conservation of momentum, perfectly inelastic collisions, recoil, and relative velocity.
